historyData.event: Laying of foundation stone of factory by the first President of India Late Dr. Rajendra Prasad.
Banaras Locomotive Works (erstwhile Diesel Locomotive works) came into existence.
First Broad Gauge Locomotive (WDM-2) released by (Late) Shri Lal Bahadur Shastri.
First Meter Gauge Locomotive (YDM-4) released by (Late) Shri Morarji Desai. (22 Nov.)
First Broad Gauge Shunting locomotive (WDS-6) turned out.
Locos exported to Tanzania.
1000th Locomotive turned out.
First Diesel Generating Set Commissioned.
Locos exported to Vietnam.
First 3100 HP WDM-2C loco turned out.
First 2300 HP WDP-1 Passenger loco turned out.
First 3100 HP WDG-2 Freight loco turned out.
Locos exported to Sri Lanka.
Locos exported to Bangladesh.
Awarded ISO-9002 Certification.
First 3100 HP WDP-2 Passenger loco turned out.
4000th Locomotive turned out.
First PKD WDG-4 Locomotive turned out.
Awarded ISO-14001 Certification.
First BLW built 4000 HP WDG-4 Loco turned out.
First BLW built 3300 HP WDG-3C Freight Loco turned out.
2.4 MW Diesel Generating Set commissioned.
Certified for OHSAS- 18001:1999
First IGBT based WDG-4 loco turned out.
5000th Locomotive turned out.
Locos exported to Mozambique.
First Dual Cab WDP-4D loco turned out.
First 5500 HP WDG-5 locomotive manufactured.
First Dual Cab WDG-4D loco turned out.
Enter into Golden Jubilee Year.
BLW bagged the 'Best Production Unit Shield 2012-13' of Indian Railways.
1000th High Horse Power Locomotive turned out.
Golden Jubilee Locomotive WDP4B 'PRATEEK' turned out.
BLW bagged the 'Best Production Unit Shield 2015-16' of Indian Railways.
First Vacuum type Bio-Toilet equipped WDG-4D loco turned out.
2000th HHP loco flagged off by MoSR.
First electric loco WAP-7 turned out.
Obtained certification of ISO 50001, for Energy Management and GreenCo Silver Rating under the GreenCo Green Company Rating System.
BLW bagged the 'Best Production Unit Shield 2016-17' of Indian Railways for second successive year.
Obtained certification of ISO 3834, for Fusion Welding of Metallic Materials.
Obtained NABL Accreditation for Laboratories.
Implementation of 5 S Workplace Management System.
BLW bagged the 'Best Production Unit Shield 2017-18' of Indian Railways for 3rd consecutive year.
Successfully converted two old HHP diesel loco (WDG4) into Electric loco WAG11.
IRIS Certification obtained.
First ever dual traction WDAP 5 locomotive turned out.
Obtained certification of ISO 50001:2018 for Energy Management System.
BLW bagged the 'Best Production Unit Shield 2020-21' of Indian Railways fifth time.
1000th Electric loco turned out.
Highest ever locomotive production of 367 in 2021-22.
Highest ever Electric locomotive Bogies Production of 52 loco set in a month.
Manufacturing of 8 shell assemblies of WAG9H with complete piping & painting in second consecutive months.
10,000th locomotive turned out.
Highest ever Electric locomotives Production of 61 in a month & highest ever locomotive production figure of 477.
11,000th locomotive turned out.

orgStrengthData.title: Organization Strength
points: Banaras Locomotive Works (BLW) is a premier locomotive manufacturing unit of Indian Railways, capable of producing locomotives with a wide range of horsepower—from 1350 HP to 6000 HP.
The facilities at BLW are equipped with state-of-the-art machinery, tools, and technologies required for manufacturing high-quality locomotives.
In line with Indian Railways' policy of 100% electrification, BLW now manufactures electric locomotives for IR and also domestic and export requirements of diesel locomotives.
State-of-the-art design and manufacturing facility to manufacture locomotives for meeting transportation needs of Indian Railways and Non-Railway Customers (NRC) & Export.
Maintenance support is provided through the supply of spares to Zonal Railways, Non-Railway Customers, and export clients as per demand.
Unbeatable trail-blazing track record in providing cost-effective, eco-friendly and reliable solutions to ever-increasing transportation needs for over six decades.
Fully geared to meet specific transportation needs by putting Price - Value - Technology equation perfectly right.

A large base of delighted customers among many countries viz. Myanmar, Sri Lanka, Malaysia, Vietnam, Bangladesh, Tanzania, Angola, Mozambique to name a few, bearing testimony to product leadership in its category.
BLW has an advanced Regional Welding Research Institute, capable of certifying welders for Indian Railways.
It also serves as a nodal center for the 'Rail Kaushal Vikas' programme of Indian Railways.

BLW has a strong focus on environmental sustainability, with initiatives like roof-mounted solar power plants, rainwater harvesting, energy-efficient lighting, tree plantations, and waste management. Approximately 40% of BLW area is green, with more than 1.2 lakh trees in the township area.
BLW is certified under ISO 9001, ISO 14001, and ISO 45001, covering integrated Quality, Environment, and Health & Safety Management Systems.

BLW has achieved ISO 50001 for Energy Management, ISO 3834 for Fusion Welding of Metallic Materials, NABL Accreditation for laboratory, 5S Workplace Management System, and IRIS for efficient and transparent system for Design & Development and Manufacturing of Locos.

heritageLocomotives.heritagePageSubtitle: List of Preserved Steam and Diesel Locomotives at Banaras Locomotive Works (BLW)

description.heading: [1] KUNDAN (YB: 3rd January 1964)
description.para1: The first BLW built Broad Gauge WDM2 locomotive no. 18233 was flagged-off by Shri Lal Bahadur Shastri on 3rd January 1964. The locomotive was then homed at Howrah diesel shed. It worked initially on freight service. Thereafter, it hauled the prestigious Kalka Mail and Amritsar Mail. It was transferred to Bardhman Diesel Shed in 1977. It worked there till the dawn of the new millennium. In 2004, it was shifted to Mughalsarai shed.
description.para2: The locomotives had two 3-axle tri-mount type Co-Co bogies.
description.para3: The locomotive was powered with a 251-B type, 16-cylinder, turbo charged, 4-stroke diesel engine capable of developing 2636 HP under SAE standard climatic conditions. The power transmission was electric, consisting of one DC main generator, Exciter and Auxiliary generator. The transmission included 6 DC traction motors, geared for a maximum speed of 120 kmph. Compressed air brake for locomotive and vacuum brake system for the train was provided. This 50-year-old locomotive has now been decommissioned and is permanent exhibit at Central Market, BLW as heritage of Indian Railways.

description.heading: [2] Miss Muffet (YB: 1935-Narrow Gauge Steam Locomotive)
description.para1: Miss Muffet is the smallest steam locomotive ever used in the country barely the size of a small lorry.
description.para2: It was manufactured by W.G. Bangal Ltd. Castle Engine Works, U.K. It was on the line in 1935 with a maximum rolling speed of 13 mph. Its coal capacity was 10 cwt and the water tank capacity of 260 gallons, when fully loaded it weighed 11.80 tones.
description.para3: The heritage steam locomotive is on display in the front of BLW Administrative Office.

description.heading: [3] Hercules (YB: 1908 Steam Locomotive)
description.para1: Hercules was manufactured by M/s Nasmyth Wilson & Co, Patricroft, Manchester, UK in 1908. It was used for shop shunting at Jamalpur Workshop. A silver chimney crowns its boiler head.
description.para2: The Locomotive is 7440 mm long, 2410 mm in width and 4110 mm in height. Its total weight is 23 tonnes, water capacity 2272 litres having one tonne of coal also.
description.para3: The heritage steam locomotive is on display in the front of BLW Administrative Office.

description.heading: [4] Mahalaxmi (YB: 1944-WDS1 Diesel Locomotive)
description.para1: Mahalaxmi, the Broad gauge diesel locomotive, was manufactured by M/s General Electric Company, U.S.A. in 1944, costing Rs. 1,86,900/- approximately. The engine was provided by M/s Cater Pillar Tractor Co., U.S.A.
description.para2: It is of 10331 mm in length, 2743 mm in width and 3760 mm in height. Its fuel capacity is 946 litres and total weight 46 tonnes.
description.para3: It was utilized in yard shunting and as goods pilot in Bombay area. This heritage diesel locomotive is on display in the front of BLW Administrative Office.

description.heading: [5] Locomotive No. 6073 (YB-1960 YDM3 Meter Gauge Diesel Electric)
description.para1: This YDM3 Class MG Diesel Locomotive was homed at Sabarmati Diesel Shed, WR. The locomotive was manufactured by M/s GM-EMD, USA.
description.para2: It was powered by single 12-cylinders, 2-stroke EMD 567C type diesel engine capable of developing 1390 hp at 800 RPM under SAE standard conditions.
description.para3: The locomotive was equipped with Electric Transmission consisting of one EMD make D-25 Direct Current main generator and two EMU make D 69 MC direct current. This heritage diesel locomotive is on display in the front of BLW Cinema Hall.

description.heading: [6] Locomotive No 6074  (YB-1960* YDM3 Meter Gauge Diesel Electric)
description.para1: This YDM3 Class MG Diesel Locomotive was homed at Sabarmati Diesel Shed, WR. The locomotive was manufactured by M/s GM-EMD, USA.
description.para2: It was powered by single 12-cylinders, 2-stroke EMD 567C type diesel engine capable of developing 1390 hp at 800 RPM under SAE standard conditions.
description.para3: The locomotive was equipped with Electric Transmission consisting of one EMD make D-25 Direct Current main generator and two EMU make D 69 MC direct current. This heritage diesel locomotive is on display in the front of BLW EDP Centre. (* Tentative)

description.heading: [7] Locomotive No. 6098 (YB-1964* YDM5 Meter Gauge Diesel Electric)
description.para1: This 5th generation YDM5 Class MG Diesel Locomotive was homed at Sabarmati Diesel Shed, WR. The locomotive was manufactured by M/s GM-EMD, USA.
description.para2: The locomotive was powered by a single 12-cylinders, 2-stroke EMD 567C type diesel engine capable of developing 1390 hp at 800 RPM under SAE standard conditions.
description.para3: The locomotive was equipped with Electric Transmission consisting of one EMD make D-25 Direct Current main generator and two EMU make D 69 MC direct current. This heritage diesel locomotive is on display in the front of BLW Design Office. (* Tentative)

quality.title: Quality Assurance
quality.subtitle: Quality Assurance
description: Quality has been a crusade in BLW since its very inception. We actively inculcate the primary importance of manufacturing a quality product in all our workmen, supervisors and engineers from the day they join BLW.
Each of our workmen is continually trained and re-trained in quality aspects. Modern instrumentation and machinery help the workmen in maintaining a high standard of quality.
Under ISO 9001 certification scheme, all our jigs and fixtures, tools and gauges are calibrated regularly according to a carefully worked out plan.
BLW has a fully equipped Gauge Room for calibration of gauges, and a Tool Room for checking of jigs and fixtures.
To supplement the rigorous standards of certification for BLW's vendors, almost all bought-out items are subjected to quality checks and certified by our inspectors.
BLW's quality thrust has been certified by an internationally accredited ISO certifying body, and BLW is a proud owner of ISO 9001 & 5-S certificate for the entire range of manufacturing activities.

milestonesFuturePlans.title: Recent Milestones & Future Plans
recentMilestones: BLW achieved a significant milestone by manufacturing 523 locomotives in current FY 2025-26, setting a new record for highest annual locomotive production. This includes 511 electric and 12 diesel locomotives for domestic and export customers.
milestonesFuturePlans.certificationsTitle: At present BLW have following certifications:
locomotiveProductionDescription: Since its inception, BLW has manufactured 11,210 locomotives (all types) till February 2026. This includes 5031 ALCO, 2467 HHP and 2878 Electric locomotives for Indian Railways, and 182 export and 643 diesel locomotives for Non-Railway Customers (NRC).
productionPlan2025: A total of 553 electric locomotives, comprising 394 WAG9H and 159 WAP7 (including 24 'Amrit Bharat') locomotives, have been planned for manufacturing in FY 2025–26 against the Railway Board target.
In addition, 17 diesel locomotives have been planned to manufacture for domestic and foreign customers.
futurePlan2026: A total of 642 electric locomotives, comprising 400 WAG9H and 242 WAP7 (including 166 'Amrit Bharat') locomotives, have been planned to manufacture in FY 2026–27 against the Railway Board target.
Further, the Railway Board has directed BLW to undertake the POH of diesel and electric locomotives.
milestonesFuturePlans.pohPlanTitle: Year-wise POH Plan up to FY 2029-30

QualityPolicy.title: Our Quality Policy
QualityPolicy.subtitle: Quality, Health, Safety & Environment (QHSE) Policy
QualityPolicy.description: Banaras Locomotive Works, a Production Unit under Ministry of Railways, is committed to manufacture reliable, cost-effective and state-of-the-art Locomotives, Diesel Generating sets, and their spares for Indian Railways, Non-Railway Customers including exports.
QualityPolicy.commitmentTitle: We are committed to achieve excellence by:
points: Enhancing Customer Satisfaction through continuous improvement in reliability, performance and safety of our products.
Comply with applicable Environmental, Health & Safety legislations and legal requirements to which our organization subscribes.
Identifying and providing relevant training to staff & supervisors and give them the opportunity to contribute for continuous improvement in Quality, Environmental, Health & Safety management system.
Communicating QHSE policy to all employees, interested parties and persons working on or behalf of the Organization.
Preventing all type of pollution by minimizing resource consumption and waste generation using cleaner technologies, material substitution and process changes.
Preventing all injuries and loss of property, including environmental performance, through continuous safety inspections.
Striving for 'Right first time' and safe working practice through system improvement and training.
Implementing progressive health and safety practices to prevent all employees from occupational diseases and health hazards.
Setting objectives & targets and periodically monitor their progress.
Ensuring failure prevention and customer expectations.

Portal.title: Portal Policies
Portal.introduction: The users of the portal would like to contact Indian Railway organisations to ask questions, get information, seek clarifications or sort out problems. Therefore it is essential that websites provide them with the means to do that.

contactUs.title: Contact Us Page
contactUs.description: All Indian Railways websites must have a 'Contact Us' page, linked from the Homepage and all relevant places in the website.
details: The 'Contact Us' page should be categorised according to the various divisions handling different kinds of queries; e.g. grievance redressal, file status, procedural details, etc.

The contact details for the important functionaries in the Department must include telephone numbers, fax numbers, postal address, as well as email address along with timings specified for personal public dealing (if applicable).
The content policy of the department should enlist the functionaries whose details are to be given on the website.
There should be a clear-cut policy for redress (correction) of inaccurate information found on the website.
The contact details of the Web Information Manager, who is overall responsible for the content on the website, should be provided.

nationalPortal.title: Presence on the National Portal
nationalPortal.description: Mechanism must be in place to ensure that all Citizen Services, Forms, Documents, and Schemes are registered with the respective repositories of the National Portal.

secondaryContent.title: Secondary Content
secondaryContent.description: Secondary content is generated from the assortment and packaging of primary content to suit the requirement of different audience, events, and occasions.

eventsAnnouncements.title: Events and Announcements
eventsAnnouncements.description: Indian Railways websites should have a section to cover various Events & Announcements such as:
examples: Announcements having National/State level importance should be published on the website.
Announcements related to important upcoming Indian Railways / Government events being organised by a relevant organisation/Ministry/Department/State/Centre.
Announcements related to schemes, grants, scholarships, fellowships, etc.
Calls for relief funds during disasters. Help from other agencies.
Display of important helpline numbers in case of crisis.
guidelines: Announcements must be taken off or archived once they lose relevance or after the expiry of the time period attached to the event or happening.
All important announcements should also be published on the National Portal for wider access.
Announcements should be worded in simple English/Regional language depending on whom it is meant for.

relatedLinks.title: Related Links
relatedLinks.description: For every content topic in different modules of the website, a section should be provided for 'Related Links' to the concerned information.
guidelines: Each content topic covered in the website may have some Related Links to other Government websites which provide further details on the topic.
For every Related Link, the complete URL of the Homepage/Concerned webpage must be provided correctly along with the complete title of the Website which shall appear on the screen.
The validity and accuracy of the URL must be checked on a regular basis to make sure that the information is relevant and the linked address is correct.
Only Government websites/web pages should be provided as 'Related Links' for further information since there is no control over the veracity and availability of information on private websites.

Department.title: Department
Department.description: The department is responsible for overseeing and managing various functions within the organization, ensuring that all operations are conducted efficiently, safely, and in accordance with applicable rules and regulations. It coordinates with other departments and stakeholders to implement policies, monitor performance, and achieve organizational objectives.

design.title: Design & Development Capabilities
design.description: Click here to view the detailed PDF.

locomotive.name: WDP3A
locomotive.subtitle: 3100 HP PASSENGER LOCOMOTIVE
description: High power-high speed dual cab Passenger Locomotive. Powered with 3100 HP, 4 stroke, fuel efficient diesel engine. Fabricated Underframe and two stage suspension, 3-axle, flexi-coil MK-V bogies, excellent riding quality.

The locomotive is powered with BLW make 251C uprated 16 cylinder fuel efficient engine with Napier NA 295 IR model or ABB VTC 304-VG 15 model turbo super charger and a large after cooler for better cooling of the engine inlet air.
The locomotive is equipped with AC-DC transmission consisting of a directly coupled self ventilated BHEL make TA10102 model traction alternator.
This engine develops 3100HP at 1050 RPM under standard condition.

dieselEngine.title: Diesel Engine
points: 16 Cylinder 251 B- (Uprated) Engine, 4 stroke, turbocharged
Fuel Efficient Engine
Injection System - Direct Injector
Governor - Woodward
Compression Ratio- 11.75:1
Lube Oil Sump Capacity - 1025 Liter

transmission.title: Transmission
points: Electrical AC-DC
6 Traction motor ( 3 in parallel per bogie)
Suspension - Axle hung / nose suspension
Gear Ratio - 22:61

truck.title: Truck
points: Fabricated flexi coil MK-V CO-CO Bogie
Adhesion - 0.25

brakes.title: Brakes
points: Panel mounted IRAB-I System
Air , hand
Pure air brake

sections.heading: Environmental Management
paragraphs: A healthy and congenial environment alone can produce and promote healthy citizens, we firmly believe. In order to have a clean and green BLW, we have a well defined integrated environmental policy. This promotes an ever motivated work-force, giving rise to products of International standard.
points: Reduction in Resource consumption
Reduction in Fire Emergencies -10% every year
Improvement in Emergency preparedness
Monitoring of water & ambient air periodically

sections.heading: Sewage Treatment Plant:
paragraphs: STP is mainly concerned with the treatment of domestic and industrial sewage. The treated water is used for irrigation purpose up to kitchen gardening at BLW premises. The digested sludge is sent to sludge drying beds, later to be used as manure. Methane gas mainly produced from the digester is collected in the gas holder and supplied to the canteen.

sections.heading: Industrial Effluent Treatment Plant:
paragraphs: IETP does the job of treatment of industrial effluent which mainly contains oil and grease. The treated water is sent to nearby villages for farming and kitchen gardening at BLW.

sections.heading: Occupational Health & Safety Management:
paragraphs: BLW is OHSAS-18001 certified since September, 2005. OHSAS formulates the work-procedures, defines hazards, assesses the risks involved therein and generates awareness regarding use of personal protective equipments at workplace. This enhances safety at work-place, reduces chances of accidents and makes workers more confident leading to increase in productivity.
points: Reduction in HOD cases -10% every year
Improvement in use of PPEs (100%)
Monitoring of water & ambient air periodically
